name: Source Fidelity appraisal for Tad Cardiovascular Endothelial Bp bundle
summary: Accepted. Bibliographic metadata, PMIDs, DOIs, titles, study designs, sample sizes, exposures, and abstract-level numeric results were checked against PubMed/NCBI records or existing project sources. The staged records correctly identify abstract-only extraction where applicable and preserve source-access limits. Full-text retrieval remains useful before any quantitative vascular synthesis, but it is not blocking for this bounded baseline claim.

findings.claim_or_issue: New source records carry matching PubMed identifiers, DOIs, publication metadata, and source URLs for the extracted cardiovascular/endothelial findings.
findings.why_it_matters: The bundle depends on differentiating direct tadalafil trials, PAH disease evidence, pooled safety evidence, and PDE5 class synthesis.

findings.claim_or_issue: Most newly staged quantitative vascular results are abstract-level extractions, even when PMC or journal full text may be available.
findings.why_it_matters: Abstract-level extraction is sufficient for a bounded qualitative baseline, but full texts are needed before pooling FMD, BP, microcirculation, or subgroup estimates.
findings.recommended_action: Retrieve full reports before any quantitative vascular synthesis or detailed endpoint-subdomain comparison.
